task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the existing ClipAction implementation: it threads `isInPinnedShotGroup`, gates pinned-group menus down to asset-state items only, removes the trailing divider when nothing follows, and no-ops right-click and stale-badge opens when no asset-state action is available. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed; focused vitest only failed on the already-known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` TimelineCanvas assertion.

Verified the existing useClipResize implementation: resize start records primary and adjacent origins under one shared transaction id, resize end computes primary then adjacent updates in memory, merges rows/meta updates, commits with exactly one `applyEdit`, and clears both ref entries. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed; `npx vitest run` still reports broad pre-existing unrelated failures plus the known TimelineCanvas pinned-group assertion.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: ClipAction threads `isInPinnedShotGroup`, hides split/mute/shot/delete sections inside pinned groups, keeps only asset-state items, suppresses the trailing divider when those hidden sections disappear, and no-ops both right-click and stale-badge clicks when no asset action can render.

useClipResize records both primary and adjacent origins at resize start under one shared transaction id, computes each clip update through the shared helper, applies primary then adjacent in memory, and commits the combined rows/meta updates through exactly one `applyEdit` before clearing both ref entries.
